system_summary: Metroscope indexes Rust codebases with LLM-generated summaries and serves a navigable code map via HTTP for editor integration.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Noti.name: NotifyChangeRequest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Noti.summary: Request payload carrying a file path for change notification.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Noti.explanation: A single-field struct wrapping a file path to notify the server that a file has been modified. It exists to give the JSON body a typed shape for deserialization. `notify_change` is its only consumer.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.name: OllamaClient
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.summary: HTTP client for a specific Ollama model at a configured base URL.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.explanation: Holds a reusable reqwest HTTP client, a base URL, and a model name for communicating with a local Ollama instance. The model field is public so callers can inspect which model was configured. All inference in the hayai subsystem flows through this struct's methods rather than hitting the Anthropic API directly.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.name: ProposeForkRequest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.summary: Request body for adding a decision fork to a quest.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.explanation: The inbound payload for creating a new decision point on a quest, carrying the quest it belongs to, the question being posed, and a list of mutually exclusive options. It's deserialized directly from the POST body with no intermediate validation layer — malformed JSON will return a 422 from axum automatically.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: xml
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Escapes special characters for safe XML/SVG embedding.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: Escapes the four characters that would break inline SVG text content or attribute values (`&`, `<`, `>`, `"`). Every user-supplied string — function names, explanations, file labels — passes through this before being embedded in the SVG output by `render_svg`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: dispatch_worker
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Creates a running worker assigned to a fork and returns its ID.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Creates a new worker record, links it to its fork by setting `fork.worker_id`, and persists the assignment. Each fork can hold at most one worker ID, so dispatching a second worker to the same fork silently overwrites the reference in the fork (though the old worker record remains in `tree.workers`). The completion counterpart is `worker_done`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: escalate
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Marks a fork escalated and appends its description to open questions.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Transitions a fork from pending to escalated when the orchestrator cannot make the decision autonomously, moving it from `pending_fork_ids` to `escalated_fork_ids` and appending the description to `context.open_questions`. This creates a persistent human-review queue that survives server restarts. The companion read endpoint is `get_pending_escalations`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::prop.name: propose_fork
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::prop.summary: Creates a new fork for a quest, persists it, and updates pending context.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::prop.explanation: Creates a new `Fork` record, links it to its parent quest, appends a log event, and adds the fork ID to the context's pending list — all in one handler. The two separate write-lock blocks (one for the tree, one for context) mean there's a brief window where the tree has the fork but context doesn't; this is fine because both are saved to disk afterward. Look at `resolve_fork` for the complementary operation.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Ac.name: Act
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Ac.summary: Groups quests under a numbered story act with a title and status.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Ac.explanation: Groups a set of quests into a named, ordered chapter of work with a completion status. Acts provide the coarse narrative structure that the orchestrator advances through sequentially; an act becomes `Active` when its predecessor completes, and `Complete` when all its quests are done. The `quest_ids` list determines the membership of each act and is used to look up entries in `Tree.quests`.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Stat.name: StationKind
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Stat.summary: Enumerates the kinds of code symbols a station can represent.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Stat.explanation: Classifies what kind of syntactic construct a Station represents. This drives how the Neovim plugin renders and filters entries in the map view — functions and methods are the primary navigation targets, while structs, traits, and enums give structural context. The indexer's tree-sitter parser is responsible for assigning the correct variant during parsing.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.name: append_log
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.summary: Appends a timestamped state event as a JSON line to `state.log`.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.explanation: Opens state.log in append-create mode and writes a single newline-terminated JSON entry for the given event. Because it opens and closes the file on every call rather than holding a handle, it is safe to call concurrently without additional locking, at the cost of repeated open syscalls. The resulting file is a standard JSON-lines format readable by any log viewer or jq pipeline.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::lo.name: load_json
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::lo.summary: Reads and deserializes a JSON file, returning `None` on any error.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::lo.explanation: A small helper that reads a file to bytes and deserializes it, returning None on any I/O or parse error rather than propagating. The Option return is intentional: callers use unwrap_or_default to treat a missing or malformed file as an empty initial state. It is only called by Store::load.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Lo.name: LogEntry
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Lo.summary: Pairs a UTC timestamp with a state event for log entries.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Lo.explanation: A timestamped wrapper around a StateEvent used to persist individual state transitions to the append-only state.log file. It exists to give the log file a human-readable and machine-parseable audit trail of what happened and when. Each entry is serialized as a single JSON line by Store::append_log.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: WorkerDoneParams
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Parameters for marking a worker complete with a summary.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Parameter struct for reporting that a worker has completed its assignment. The `summary` field captures what the worker found or did, which gets stored on the worker record and logged as a state event. The primary consumer is `HayaiMcpServer::worker_done`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::G.name: GenerateResponse
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::G.summary: Wraps the text response from Ollama's generate endpoint.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::G.explanation: A minimal deserialization target for the response field from Ollama's generate endpoint, discarding all other returned metadata such as token counts and timing. This narrow shape keeps the client decoupled from Ollama's full response schema. It is only used inside OllamaClient::generate.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::i.name: index_project
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::i.summary: Parses Rust files, generates LLM summaries, and writes the index JSON.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::i.explanation: The central orchestrator for a full index run. It loads an existing index as an incremental cache, walks all non-ignored Rust files, parses them with tree-sitter via `RustParser`, calls the LLM in batches for summaries, builds `Station` and `Line` maps, optionally enriches them with Serena's `CalledBy` edges, serializes the result to `.metroscope/index.json`, and prints a token-cost report. The Serena path is the only async branch that can be skipped at runtime.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::sa.name: save_json
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::sa.summary: Serializes a value to pretty JSON and writes it to the given path.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::sa.explanation: Serializes a value to pretty-printed JSON and atomically overwrites the target file. Pretty printing is chosen for human readability of the on-disk files over compactness. It is called by both save_tree and save_context, and is the single write path for all persistent state.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: NotifyChangeParams
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Parameters carrying the file path that changed.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Parameter struct carrying a single file path to signal that a file was modified. It exists so the server can maintain a recency-ordered list of touched files in the active context. The primary consumer is `HayaiMcpServer::notify_change`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.name: Store
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.summary: Holds persistent state: directory path, tree, and context behind read-write locks.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.explanation: The central persistence handle for the hayai subsystem, owning both the project tree and the conversation context behind async-safe read-write locks. It wraps a `.metro/` directory and provides the only path through which state is read from or written to disk. All MCP tool handlers receive a shared Arc<Store> so they coordinate through a single instance.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: get_tree
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Returns the full skill tree state as JSON.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Returns the full skill tree (all acts, quests, forks, and workers) as a single JSON blob. This is the ground-truth read endpoint when `get_context` isn't sufficient — useful for debugging or when a caller needs the complete picture. Because it serializes the entire tree under a write lock, callers should prefer `get_quest` for targeted lookups.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.name: build_quests_prompt
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.summary: Builds a prompt requesting 3-7 architectural improvement quests for the system.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.explanation: Builds the prompt that asks Claude to identify 3-7 architectural improvement opportunities, deliberately restricting scope to system-level concerns to avoid noise from style suggestions. It uses the already-computed system summary and per-crate summaries as context. The response is consumed by `parse_quests`.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Stat.name: Station
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Stat.summary: Represents a single code symbol with location, summaries, connections, and metadata.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Stat.explanation: The central data model — one Station per indexed symbol. It bundles source location, LLM-generated prose, call graph edges, and an FNV-1a body hash in one place so both the server and plugin can work entirely from the deserialized index without re-reading source files. The `body_hash` is the key to incremental re-indexing: if the hash hasn't changed, the indexer skips the LLM call for that function. The most important consumer is the server's `/station/*id` endpoint.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: CrateBox
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Stores layout geometry and index for a crate panel in the SVG.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: A local layout record that stores the computed position, size, and palette index for one crate panel. It is created during the layout pass of `render_svg` and consumed immediately afterward in the draw pass to emit the rounded-rectangle background and header bar.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Reso.name: ResolveForkRequest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Reso.summary: Request body to resolve a fork with a chosen option and optional reason.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Reso.explanation: The inbound payload for closing a fork by recording which option was chosen and an optional human-readable reason. The `reason` field is intentionally optional to allow automated resolutions (e.g., from a worker) that don't need a justification string.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Work.name: WorkerDoneRequest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Work.summary: Request payload carrying a worker ID and its completion summary.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Work.explanation: Carries the worker ID and a free-text summary back to the server when an external worker finishes its assignment. The summary is stored on the worker record and also written to the event log for auditability. `worker_done` is its sole consumer.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Line.name: Line
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Line.summary: Represents a source file with its display name, color, summary, and ordered station ids.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Line.explanation: Represents a source file as a named, colored "line" on the metro map. The `color` string is pre-assigned at index time so the renderer never has to derive it. `stations` holds IDs in source order, which lets the map view lay them out spatially without sorting. The Neovim `render.lua` module reads these fields directly when drawing the map.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ll.name: LlmBackend
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ll.summary: Selects between direct API access with a key or delegating to the Claude CLI.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ll.explanation: Abstracts over two ways to reach Claude so the indexer can work both with a raw API key and in environments where the `claude` CLI is already authenticated. The `Cli` variant trades token-count visibility (it returns zeros) for zero-configuration convenience. The `call_claude` dispatcher is the only place that branches on this enum.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.name: ApiMessage
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.summary: Holds a single role-content pair for a Claude API message.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.explanation: Represents a single turn in the messages array required by the Anthropic API format. The indexer always sends exactly one user message per call, so in practice this struct is instantiated once per `ApiRequest`. The `role` field exists to satisfy the API schema even though only `"user"` is ever used here.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.name: ApiUsage
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.summary: Records input and output token counts from a Claude API response.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.explanation: Holds the input and output token counts from an API response for cost and quota tracking. These values are propagated up through `call_via_api` as the second and third tuple elements, then accumulated into `Summaries.tokens_in` and `tokens_out`. The `Cli` backend always returns zeros here since the CLI doesn't expose usage data.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::work.name: worker_done
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::work.summary: Marks worker done, resolves its fork, and completes the quest if all forks resolved.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::work.explanation: Marks a worker as `Done`, records its summary and finish timestamp, and then checks whether resolving this worker's parent fork causes all forks in the parent quest to be resolved — if so, the quest is automatically promoted to `Complete`. The entire tree mutation happens under a single write-lock acquisition to keep the resolution check atomic. After persisting, it calls `rebuild_context` to synchronize the derived context snapshot with the new tree state.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.name: disambiguate_name
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.summary: Derives a display name from a file path, prepending the crate name to avoid collisions.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.explanation: Derives a human-readable display name for a file by combining the crate name and file stem, stripping the common `metroscope-` prefix from the crate name. The edge case it handles is that `src/` directories are transparent — it steps up one more level to find the crate name. The result becomes `ParsedFile::file_name`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.name: ForkStatus
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.summary: Tracks a fork's resolution state from pending through escalated or resolved.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.explanation: Tracks the lifecycle of a decision point from first proposal to resolution. The distinction between `AutoResolved` and `Escalated` encodes the key routing decision: the orchestrator can resolve simple forks itself, but forks it cannot confidently decide get pushed to `Escalated` so a human can intervene via the `Fork`'s `chosen_option_id`.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.name: handle_station
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.summary: Returns full detail for a station by ID, with resolved connections.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.explanation: Handles GET `/station/:id` by looking up the station, resolving each raw connection to a full `ResolvedConnection` by joining against the stations map, and returning a `StationDetail`. Connections that don't resolve to a known station are silently dropped rather than shown as stubs, keeping the response clean. The most important callee conceptually is `index.stations.get`, and the most important data type is `StationDetail`.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.name: extract_calls
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.summary: Extracts deduplicated callee names from all call expressions in a function node.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.explanation: Runs a tree-sitter query over a function's subtree to collect all called identifiers, covering direct calls, method calls, and path-qualified calls. Results are sorted and deduplicated so the list is stable across re-parses. These names are stored in `ParsedFunction::calls` and later resolved to full station IDs in the server's map-building logic.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.name: save_tree
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.explanation: Acquires a read lock on the tree and serializes it to tree.json, using a read (not write) lock because saving doesn't mutate in-memory state. This is the counterpart to the tree half of Store::load. It is called by MCP tool handlers after any mutation to the tree to keep the on-disk file consistent.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.name: CallerRef
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.summary: Holds a caller's symbol name path and source file path.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.explanation: A plain data container that records where a caller lives — its symbolic name path and the file it belongs to. It exists because Serena's MCP response groups symbols by file and kind, so both pieces of information are needed to reconstruct a station ID. The main consumer is `enrich`, which converts these into `CalledBy` connections in `index_project`.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: text
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Renders an SVG `<text>` element with given position and style.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: Analogous to `rect` but for `<text>` elements; it handles font size, weight, anchor, and an `extra` slot used for things like `font-style="italic"` or `letter-spacing`. All visible labels in the SVG go through this function.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Re.name: ResolvedConnection
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Re.summary: Holds resolved metadata for a single call or called-by connection.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Re.explanation: A flattened, response-ready version of a connection that embeds the target station's name, summary, and location directly rather than just an ID. It exists because the raw `Connection` type in the index only stores the target ID, so the handler must look up and inline the station details for the client. Used exclusively inside `handle_station`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: ProposeForkParams
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Parameters for proposing a decision fork on a quest with multiple options.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Parameter struct for the propose_fork tool, tying a fork to its parent quest and carrying the full decision question alongside a list of ProposeForkOptionParam entries. The handler that receives this creates a Fork in the store's context and may immediately attempt auto-resolution via OllamaClient::try_resolve_fork.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.name: call_via_api
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.summary: Sends a prompt to the Claude API and returns the response text and token counts.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.explanation: Sends a prompt to the Anthropic Messages API over HTTPS and returns the response text plus token counts. It constructs a minimal `ApiRequest`, checks for a non-2xx status before attempting JSON deserialization (avoiding a confusing parse error on error bodies), and joins multi-block content into a single string. The only caller is `call_claude`.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Ma.name: MapParams
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Ma.summary: Query parameters for the map endpoint: file, line, and crate filter.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Ma.explanation: Query parameter struct for the `/map` endpoint, accepting an optional file path, line number, and crate filter. The `crate_filter` field is renamed from `crate` (a reserved keyword in Rust) using a serde rename attribute. All fields are optional so the endpoint degrades gracefully to a module-level view when no cursor position is provided.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: StationLayout
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Stores computed x/y position, dimensions, and center-y for a station box.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: Holds the computed bounding box and vertical center point for one station in the SVG layout pass. It is populated during the layout loop in `render_svg` and then read back when drawing boxes and routing arrow endpoints.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: ResolveForkParams
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Parameters for resolving a fork by selecting a chosen option with optional reason.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Parameter struct carrying the data needed to mark a decision fork as resolved. It holds which fork to close, which option was chosen, and an optional human-readable rationale. The `reason` field is optional because some resolutions are self-evident and don't require justification. The primary consumer is `HayaiMcpServer::resolve_fork`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: ProposeForkOptionParam
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Describes one fork option with label, description, and what it unlocks or forecloses.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Represents one option within a fork proposal, capturing its human-readable label, a description of what choosing it means, what it unlocks, and what it forecloses. The unlocks and forecloses fields make the consequences of each option explicit so both the AI resolver and a human reviewer can reason about trade-offs without reading surrounding context.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.name: body_hash
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.summary: Returns an FNV-1a hex hash of the function body for cache invalidation.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.explanation: Computes an FNV-1a hash of the function body to detect source changes between index runs. It returns a hex string so the hash is human-readable in the stored index cache. `generate_summaries` compares this against the cached hash to decide whether to skip an LLM call.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.name: parse_referencing_symbols
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.summary: Parses Serena's JSON response into a list of CallerRef entries.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.explanation: Deserializes the JSON blob that Serena returns from `find_referencing_symbols`, which is a nested object keyed first by file path and then by symbol kind. It flattens that structure into a `Vec<CallerRef>`, pulling only the `name_path` field from each symbol entry. If the JSON is malformed the whole call returns an error, but that error is caught and ignored by `enrich`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.name: MetroDifficulty
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.summary: Enum of three difficulty levels: Easy, Medium, Hard.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.explanation: A simple three-level effort classification for quests, used only during bootstrap to annotate quest descriptions before they're persisted. It has no runtime effect after init — difficulty is baked into the description string and not stored as a structured field on `TreeQuest`. See `as_str` for serialization.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::pa.name: parse_function_batch_response
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::pa.summary: Parses `id|summary` lines from LLM response into a map, filling missing entries with a default.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::pa.explanation: Parses the `id|summary` line-oriented response format from Claude and populates the output map, filtering out any IDs not in the current batch to avoid cross-contamination. Functions that produce no matching line in the response are given a fallback value of "No summary available." so downstream code never sees a missing entry. Both `generate_summaries` call sites (summaries and explanations) use this same parser.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::reso.name: resolve_fork
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::reso.summary: Marks a fork as resolved, records the decision, and removes it from pending context.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::reso.explanation: Marks a fork as resolved with the chosen option and removes it from both the pending and escalated lists in context, so a single call handles both normal and escalated resolution paths. The `resolved_at` timestamp is set here rather than in `propose_fork`, giving an accurate measure of decision latency. It persists both tree and context to disk before returning.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Pr.name: PromptOverrides
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Pr.summary: Holds optional custom prompt strings for function, file, and system summarization.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Pr.explanation: Lets callers substitute custom prompt templates for any of the three generation phases — per-function, per-file, or system-level. All fields are `Option` so the caller only needs to supply the overrides they care about; the indexer falls back to built-in defaults for `None` fields. This struct feeds into the summarization functions that build the actual prompts sent to `call_claude`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Disp.name: DispatchWorkerResponse
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Disp.summary: Response payload returning the newly assigned worker ID.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Disp.explanation: A minimal response struct that echoes back the newly generated worker ID so the caller can reference the worker in future calls such as `worker_done`. Keeping it as a typed struct rather than an ad-hoc JSON value ensures the field name stays stable across serialization.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: get_pending_escalations
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Returns all forks currently in escalated status.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Reads all forks whose status is `Escalated` directly from the tree and returns them. It exists as a convenient polling endpoint for human review tooling without requiring callers to filter the full tree themselves. Note it reads from `store.tree` rather than `context.escalated_fork_ids`, so it reflects the authoritative state rather than the cached summary.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.name: handle_export_svg
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.summary: Renders the index as an SVG and returns it with the correct content type.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.explanation: Handles GET `/export/svg` by calling `export::render_svg` and returning the result with an explicit `image/svg+xml` content-type header. Unlike JSON handlers, this returns a raw string with a manually set header tuple rather than using the `Json` extractor. The interesting logic lives entirely in `export::render_svg`.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Lin.name: LineView
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Lin.summary: A line's display data including its stations and focus state.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Lin.explanation: A response-ready representation of a metro line, carrying only the fields needed by the client plus an `is_focused` flag so the renderer can highlight the active line without doing its own lookup. The `stations` vector here is a windowed subset, not the full line — see `build_map_response` for the windowing logic.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::C.name: Cli
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::C.summary: Top-level CLI struct holding the parsed subcommand.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::C.explanation: The top-level clap parser struct; it exists only to dispatch to a subcommand. The single subcommand today is `Index`, so in practice this is just a thin wrapper that routes to `Command`.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.name: call_claude
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.summary: Dispatches a prompt to Claude via API or CLI based on the configured backend.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.explanation: The single dispatch point for all LLM calls — it routes to either the HTTP API or the CLI backend and normalizes the return type to a `(text, tokens_in, tokens_out)` tuple. Centralizing the dispatch here means the rest of the indexer never needs to know which backend is active. Its two callees are `call_via_api` and `call_via_cli`.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ma.name: main
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ma.summary: Starts the HTTP server, loads the index, and registers all routes.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ma.explanation: Sets up the entire server: loads the index, spawns the file watcher as a background task, registers all HTTP routes with shared `AppState` (an `Arc<RwLock<Index>>`), and starts listening. The `AppState` wrapping is the key architectural decision here — it allows the watcher to swap the index in place without restarting the server. The routes delegate to handler functions, which in turn delegate to `map::build_map_response` and related logic.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.name: build_file_connections
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.summary: Builds cross-file call and called-by connections for all stations in a file.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.explanation: Scans all stations belonging to a given file and collects every connection that crosses into a different file, grouping them by the target file's ID into two maps: what this file calls into versus what calls into this file. The resolution logic has a fallback: if the connection target isn't found by its full ID, it tries to match by bare function name across all other files, which handles cases where the index stores unqualified names. The resulting `FileConnections` is served by the `/connections` endpoint and drives the cross-file dependency view in the Neovim plugin.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: line_el
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Renders an SVG `<line>` element with given endpoints and stroke.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: Builds an SVG `<line>` element string, used for the horizontal dividers inside station boxes and for the legend arrows at the bottom of the diagram. The `extra` slot carries the `marker-end` attribute on legend lines.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Cl.name: Cli
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Cl.summary: CLI args for the server: index file path and port number.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Cl.explanation: Holds the two server startup parameters: the path to the index file and the port to listen on (defaulting to 7777). It is parsed once at startup and the values are passed directly into the axum server setup; there is no dynamic reconfiguration.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.name: build_map_response
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.summary: Builds a windowed map response centered on the focused station.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.explanation: The core map-building function that filters lines by crate, sorts them, windows each line's station list around the focused station using a context radius, and pre-computes `is_focused` and `has_cross_line` for every station in the window. The windowing is the key detail: clients only receive a slice of nearby stations, not the full line, to keep responses small. The most important callees are `station_has_cross_line` and `crate_id_of`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: resolve_fork
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Marks a fork resolved with the chosen option and removes it from pending.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Marks a fork as resolved with the chosen option and an optional reason, then removes the fork ID from both `pending_fork_ids` and `escalated_fork_ids` in the context. It handles the case where a fork was escalated and then manually resolved by a human, cleaning up both lists. Persistence is written to disk after every state mutation; the next place to look is `worker_done`, which also resolves forks but via a different code path.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.name: build_function_batch_prompt
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.summary: Builds a prompt requesting one-line summaries for a batch of functions.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.explanation: Constructs the prompt sent to Claude for generating one-line summaries of a batch of functions, formatting each function as a fenced code block preceded by its ID. The caller can inject a custom system prefix via `override_prefix`, otherwise a default instruction is used. The response is consumed by `parse_function_batch_response`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::ser.name: serve
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::ser.summary: Loads the store, wires MCP and HTTP routes, and starts the axum server.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::ser.explanation: Bootstraps and runs the full Hayai server, wiring together two parallel interfaces: a plain HTTP API (used by CLI hooks) and a proper MCP server over Streamable HTTP (used by Claude Code via .mcp.json). Both share the same `Store` via `Arc`, but only the HTTP routes get the `OllamaClient` — the MCP server works with the store directly. The key gotcha is that the `/mcp` prefix is handled by `StreamableHttpService` while all other routes go through `mcp::router`, so they live on the same port but are dispatched differently. Look next at `mcp::router` for the full HTTP API surface.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Wo.name: WorkerStatus
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Wo.summary: Indicates whether a worker is running, finished successfully, or failed.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Wo.explanation: A simple three-state enum for a spawned agent's lifecycle: `Running` while executing, `Done` on success, `Failed` on error. The orchestrator watches for `StateEvent::WorkerDone` to transition this and then inspects the `summary` to decide the next fork or quest action.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::fir.name: first_sentence
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::fir.summary: Extracts the first sentence from a string, trimming trailing whitespace.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::fir.explanation: Extracts just the first sentence from a longer summary string by finding the earliest `. ` or `.\n` boundary. It's used to produce a concise Act 1 title from the potentially multi-sentence system summary without pulling in a full NLP library. If no sentence boundary is found, it returns the entire string, so it never panics on short inputs.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: word_wrap
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Wraps text into lines not exceeding a given column width.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: Breaks a string into lines that fit within a given column width by splitting on whitespace and accumulating words greedily. It always returns at least one element (an empty string for empty input) so callers can safely multiply by line height without a bounds check. It is used in `render_svg` to size station boxes and to emit explanation text lines.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Fil.name: FileConnections
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Fil.summary: Cross-file connection summary with inbound and outbound file links.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Fil.explanation: The response shape for `/connections`, grouping cross-file relationships into `calls_into` and `called_from` lists. Each list contains `FileLink` entries rather than raw station IDs, so the client gets file-level granularity with enough detail to render labeled edges. Built by `build_file_connections`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: propose_fork
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Creates a new pending fork with options and registers it in the quest.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Creates a new decision fork, registers it on its parent quest, appends a log event, and adds the fork ID to `context.pending_fork_ids`. Options are assigned stable IDs like `option_0`, `option_1` based on position, so callers must refer to options by that positional ID when calling `resolve_fork`. Both the tree and context are persisted to disk before returning the new fork's ID.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.name: RustParser
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.summary: Wraps a tree-sitter `Language` handle for Rust parsing.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.explanation: A thin wrapper holding a `tree_sitter::Language` handle for Rust, used to avoid re-initializing the grammar on every parse call. It implements the `LanguageParser` trait, so the indexer can swap parsers by language. `RustParser::new` and `LanguageParser::parse` are its only two meaningful entry points.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.name: is_relevant
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.summary: Asks the model if a changed file is relevant to the active quest or pending forks.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.explanation: Asks the local model with a yes/no prompt whether a changed file matters to the current quest or pending fork decisions. It short-circuits to false when there is no active context, avoiding unnecessary inference calls. The result is used by the file-watcher layer to decide whether a change should surface to the user or be silently ignored.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: get_context
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Returns the current session context as JSON.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Returns the current orchestration context (active quest, pending forks, escalations, recently touched files) as JSON. It exists so a Claude session can immediately orient itself at the start of a conversation by calling this first, as the server's own instructions recommend. The context is a derived summary rebuilt by `rebuild_context` rather than ground truth — the canonical data lives in `store.tree`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: get_info
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Returns server metadata and usage instructions for the Hayai MCP server.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Declares the server's MCP capabilities and embeds a natural-language instruction string that tells Claude how to use the tool suite correctly. The instructions act as a system prompt for the tool layer, directing callers to start with `get_context` and use the fork/worker lifecycle in the intended order. This is the entry point that MCP clients call during capability negotiation.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: EscalateParams
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Parameters for escalating a fork to human review with a description.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Parameter struct for escalating a fork that the orchestrator cannot resolve autonomously. It pairs a fork ID with a free-text description of why the decision is blocked, which gets appended to `context.open_questions` so a human can review it. The primary consumer is `HayaiMcpServer::escalate`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::Cli.name: Cli
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::Cli.summary: Top-level CLI struct holding the subcommand.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::Cli.explanation: Top-level CLI struct that delegates entirely to the `Command` enum via clap's subcommand derive. It has no fields of its own; its only purpose is to be the root parse target passed to `Cli::parse()` in `main`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::G.name: GenerateRequest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::G.summary: Request payload for Ollama's generate endpoint with model, prompt, and stream flag.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::G.explanation: A private serialization struct that maps exactly onto Ollama's /api/generate request body. The stream field is always set to false by OllamaClient::generate, meaning the full response is collected in a single HTTP response rather than streamed. It is only used inside generate and has no independent lifecycle.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::i.name: is_ignored
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::i.summary: Returns true if a path contains `target`, `.git`, or `.metroscope`.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::i.explanation: A simple path filter that skips any path containing a `target`, `.git`, or `.metroscope` component during the file walk. It prevents the indexer from recursing into build artifacts or its own output, and is called inline inside the `WalkDir` filter closure in `index_project`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::Com.name: Command
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::Com.summary: Enum of CLI subcommands: serve, init, and notify-change.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::Com.explanation: Defines the three operating modes of the hayai binary: `Serve` starts the MCP server, `Init` bootstraps a skill tree from an existing Metroscope index, and `NotifyChange` is a lightweight client-mode call intended to be invoked from an editor hook. The `Serve` subcommand exposes Ollama configuration flags, indicating that background orchestration is intended to run against a local model rather than the Anthropic API.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: HayaiMcpServer
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: MCP server that wraps shared store access for hayai tool handlers.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: The MCP server struct that owns the shared store and acts as the handler registry for all tool calls coming in over the MCP protocol. It wraps the store in an Arc so it can be cloned cheaply into each async handler. Every tool method on this struct reads or mutates state exclusively through the Store API.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: notify_change
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Records a file change and prepends it to the recently-touched-files list.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Records a file-change event to the state log and prepends the file to a capped, ordered list of recently touched files in the context, keeping at most 10 entries. The deduplication-then-prepend pattern ensures the most recently changed file is always at index zero without duplicates. This is intended to be called from a PostToolUse hook rather than interactively.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.name: MetroQuest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.summary: Represents a single quest with title, component, rationale, and difficulty.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.explanation: Represents a single entry-point task extracted from the metroscope index, pairing a human-readable title and rationale with a component name and effort estimate. It maps one-to-one to a `TreeQuest` in the persistent store, with `why` becoming the quest description. The `difficulty` field drives the string appended to the description in `run`.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Map.name: MapResponse
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Map.summary: Response payload for the map endpoint with lines and focus metadata.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Map.explanation: The top-level response shape for the `/map` endpoint, carrying the focused station ID, the windowed list of lines with their stations, a system-level summary string, the project root path, and the focused crate ID. The `focused_crate` field is how the Neovim plugin knows which crate to highlight or filter to on startup. Built by `build_map_response`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Tr.name: TreeQuest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Tr.summary: Represents a quest node with dependencies, unlocks, and fork references.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Tr.explanation: The core unit of planned work in the hayai system, representing a discrete task with explicit dependency edges (`depends_on`) and unlock edges (`unlocks`) that form a DAG over quests. The `fork_ids` field links to any decision points that arose during the quest's execution, and `act` ties it back into the chapter structure. The orchestrator walks the DAG using `depends_on` to determine when a quest transitions from `Locked` to `Available`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::esca.name: escalate_fork
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::esca.summary: Marks a fork as escalated and moves it from pending to escalated context.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::esca.explanation: Marks a fork as `Escalated`, moves it from the pending list to the escalated list in the shared context, and appends the fork's description as an open question requiring human attention. The write lock on the tree and the write lock on the context are taken in separate blocks to avoid holding both simultaneously, which prevents potential deadlock. After this handler runs, `get_escalations` will surface the fork to whoever is polling for blockers.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Ac.name: ActStatus
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Ac.summary: Tracks whether an act is locked, currently active, or completed.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Ac.explanation: A three-state enum tracking whether an act in the quest tree is not yet reachable (`Locked`), currently in progress (`Active`), or finished (`Complete`). It is a field on `Act` and is checked by the orchestrator to determine which quests are eligible to run.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::pa.name: parse_quests
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::pa.summary: Parses `component|difficulty|title|why` lines into a list of `Quest` structs.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::pa.explanation: Parses the `component|difficulty|title|why` pipe-delimited response into `Quest` structs, defaulting unrecognized difficulty strings to `Medium` to be lenient with model output. It silently skips malformed lines rather than erroring. The resulting vec is stored in the `Summaries` struct and eventually written into the index JSON.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::m.name: main
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::m.summary: Parses CLI args and dispatches to `index_project`.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::m.explanation: Parses CLI arguments, decides which `LlmBackend` to use (direct API vs the `claude` CLI), assembles prompt overrides, and delegates everything to `index_project`. It contains no indexing logic itself; it is purely wiring.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: GetQuestParams
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Parameter carrying a quest ID for quest-lookup MCP calls.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Parameter struct for the get_quest tool, carrying the ID of the quest to look up. The single string field maps directly to what the MCP client sends; the handler reads it and queries the store's context for a matching quest.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Inde.name: station_at
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Inde.summary: Finds the station whose source range contains the given file and line number.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Inde.explanation: Finds the station whose source range contains a given line number, used by the server to map a cursor position to a station. It does a linear scan over all stations, which is acceptable given typical index sizes but would need an interval tree if projects grew very large. The primary caller is the server's `/map` endpoint, which receives a file path and line number from the editor plugin.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Co.name: Context
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Co.summary: Tracks current execution context including active quest, pending forks, and open questions.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Co.explanation: A lightweight, frequently-updated summary of what the orchestrator is currently focused on, separate from the full `Tree` so it can be read cheaply without deserializing every quest and fork. `pending_fork_ids` and `escalated_fork_ids` are the actionable queues the orchestrator drains on each tick, `recently_touched_files` helps it correlate `StateEvent::FileChanged` events, and `open_questions` captures unresolved ambiguities surfaced during work.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.name: handle_quests
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.summary: Returns the list of quests from the current index.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.explanation: Handles GET `/quests` by cloning and returning the `quests` field directly from the index. It's the simplest handler in the server — no filtering, no joins, just a read-lock and a clone. The `quests` field is populated at index time by the indexer, not the server.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.name: Fork
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.summary: Represents a decision point in a quest with options, status, and resolution metadata.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.explanation: Represents a decision point that arose mid-quest, containing the question text, the available options, the resolution status, and optionally which worker triggered it. When `status` is `Escalated`, the hayai UI presents this fork for human input; when `Resolved`, `chosen_option_id` records which `ForkOption` was picked and the orchestrator can continue dispatching work. `StateEvent::ForkResolved` is the event that closes it out.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: DispatchWorkerParams
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Parameters for dispatching a worker to a fork with instructions.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Parameter struct for spawning a worker agent to investigate or implement a specific fork. The `instructions` field is the prompt or task description handed to that worker. The primary consumer is `HayaiMcpServer::dispatch_worker`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::rout.name: router
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::rout.summary: Builds the axum router with all hayai HTTP endpoints wired to handlers.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::rout.explanation: Declares all HTTP routes and binds them to the shared `AppState`. It's called from `serve` to build the plain HTTP layer that lives alongside the MCP endpoint. The route layout maps cleanly to the tool list advertised in `manifest` — if you add a new tool, both places need updating.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Fil.name: FileLink
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Fil.summary: A linked file with its color and specific cross-line connections.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Fil.explanation: Represents one end of a cross-file connection, carrying the target file's ID, display name, color (inherited from its line), and a list of specific `CrossConnection` entries. The color field lets the Neovim renderer use the same color coding as the map view without a separate lookup.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.name: ApiRequest
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.summary: Serializes a Claude API chat request with model, token limit, and messages.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.explanation: The JSON body sent to the Anthropic Messages API. It only includes the three fields the indexer actually uses — model, token limit, and messages — keeping serialization minimal. `call_via_api` constructs one of these per LLM call and passes it directly to `reqwest`'s `.json()` serializer.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.name: call_via_cli
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.summary: Spawns the `claude` CLI subprocess, pipes a prompt to stdin, and returns stdout.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ca.explanation: Shells out to the `claude` CLI binary as a fallback LLM backend, piping the prompt via stdin and capturing stdout as the response. It uses `--no-session-persistence` and `-p` to force stateless, non-interactive mode. The most important caller is `call_claude`, which dispatches to this function when the backend is set to CLI mode rather than direct API.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::cra.name: crate_display_name
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::cra.summary: Returns the portion of a crate ID after the first hyphen as display name.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::cra.explanation: Strips the `metroscope-` prefix (or any other common prefix) from a crate ID so the UI shows a shorter, friendlier name like `"indexer"` instead of `"metroscope-indexer"`. It splits on the first hyphen and returns the remainder, which keeps the display compact in the module map. Called inside `build_module_map` when constructing each `ModuleNode`.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.name: build_system_prompt
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.summary: Builds a prompt requesting a one-sentence project summary from all file summaries.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.explanation: Assembles the project-level summary prompt by listing every file and its previously generated per-file summary. It intentionally runs after the file-summary loop so the per-file context is already available. The result feeds `generate_summaries`'s system summary step and then indirectly into `build_quests_prompt`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Tr.name: Tree
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Tr.summary: Top-level state container holding all acts, quests, forks, and workers.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Tr.explanation: The complete, persistent state of the hayai planning system — the single source of truth for all acts, quests, forks, and workers. It is the document that gets serialized to disk and reloaded across sessions. The orchestrator reads and mutates this structure by applying `StateEvent`s, and the UI renders it to show progress.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Conn.name: Connection
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Conn.summary: Links a station to another station with a directed call relationship.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Conn.explanation: Represents a directed edge between two stations in the call graph. The `to` field holds the full station ID string, so the server can resolve it directly against `Index.stations` without any secondary lookup structure. The `kind` field lets callers filter for outbound calls versus inbound references in a single pass over `connections`.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::C.name: Command
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::C.summary: Defines the `index` subcommand with path, API key, and prompt options.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs::C.explanation: Defines the `index` subcommand and all its flags, including the optional API key, the presence-only `--serena-dir` opt-in, and prompt override strings. The `serena_dir` value is intentionally ignored after parsing — only its presence matters, which is why the doc comment says "pass any value".

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: EmptyParams
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Empty parameter struct for MCP tool calls that take no arguments.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: A zero-field deserialization target for MCP tool calls that carry no parameters, used so the tool dispatch machinery has a uniform type to deserialize into. It exists purely to satisfy the generic parameter requirement of the MCP SDK's tool registration interface.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Cra.name: CrateData
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Cra.summary: Accumulates per-crate station count, color, and cross-crate connection sets.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Cra.explanation: A local accumulator struct used only inside `build_module_map` to collect mutable per-crate state (station count, color, cross-crate edge sets) before the final `ModuleNode` values are frozen and sorted. It is never exposed outside the function; once iteration is complete, each `CrateData` is destructured into a `ModuleNode`.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.name: enrich
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.summary: Spawns Serena MCP server and queries call graph for all function IDs.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.explanation: Spawns the Serena MCP server as a child process over stdio using `uvx` (so no local install is required), then queries it once per function ID using `find_referencing_symbols`. It accumulates the results into a `SerenaIndex` and returns it; errors for individual functions are logged and skipped rather than aborting the whole run. The text payload from each tool call is decoded by `parse_referencing_symbols` before being stored.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::run.name: run
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::run.summary: Bootstraps Act 1 by converting indexer quests into tree quests and persisting them.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::run.explanation: The one-shot bootstrap that converts a metroscope `index.json` into a live Hayai skill tree — creating quests, Act 1, and seeding the context with the first quest as active. It guards against accidental re-runs by bailing if quests already exist, and it appends a `ReIndexed` log event for audit purposes. The critical path flows through `Store::load`, the `tree.write()` block, `store.save_tree()`, and finally `store.save_context()`.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.name: extract_functions
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.summary: Extracts all structs, enums, functions, and methods from a tree-sitter parse tree.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.explanation: Walks the tree-sitter CST with two separate queries — one for structs/enums, one for functions/methods — and builds a `ParsedFunction` for each item found. It uses a `seen_ids` set to deduplicate in case the grammar produces overlapping matches, and calls `impl_type_name` to detect method ownership and `extract_calls` to build the call graph. The sorted output becomes `ParsedFile::functions`.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Loca.name: Location
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Loca.summary: Stores a file path with start and end line numbers for a code symbol.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Loca.explanation: Represents a source file location by file path and line range. It exists so every Station can point back to where in the source it came from, enabling the editor plugin to jump to definitions. The `line_end` field is what allows `station_at` to do range-based lookup rather than exact-line matching.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.name: ParsedFile
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.summary: Holds a parsed source file with its ID, display name, raw source, and extracted functions.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.explanation: Groups all parsed items from one source file together with the raw source text and a display name. The `source` field is kept around solely for file-level summarization in `build_file_prompt`; it is not written to the final index. `LanguageParser::parse` produces these.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.name: build_module_map
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.summary: Builds a crate-level module map with cross-crate call edges from the index.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::bui.explanation: Builds the full crate-level dependency graph by first aggregating per-line station counts into per-crate buckets, then walking every station's connections to populate cross-crate `calls_into` / `called_from` sets using `HashSet` for deduplication. The same fallback name-matching logic used in `build_file_connections` applies here for unresolved connection targets. The finished `ModuleMap` is returned directly to the `/module-map` route handler and serialized to JSON for the Neovim module-map zoom level.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::wa.name: watch_index
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::wa.summary: Watches index.json for changes and hot-reloads it into shared state.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::wa.explanation: Watches the parent directory of index.json for file system events and hot-reloads the index whenever a write or create event fires. It watches the directory rather than the file directly to handle atomic rename-into-place writes (common in tools that write to a temp file then rename). A 100ms sleep after each notification lets the writer finish flushing before reading. Multiple rapid events are drained from the channel to avoid redundant reloads. The key callee is `load_index`.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: rect
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Renders an SVG `<rect>` element with given geometry and style.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: A thin SVG string builder for `<rect>` elements that centralizes attribute ordering and escaping. The `extra` parameter allows callers to inject one-off attributes like `filter="url(#sh)"` without needing separate builder variants.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: crate_id_of
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Extracts crate name from a `crates/<name>/...` line ID.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: Extracts the crate name from a line ID formatted as `crates/<name>/...`, returning `"root"` for any path that does not follow that convention. It is used in `render_svg` both to group lines into crate panels and to decide whether a call arrow is cross-crate.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::St.name: StationDetail
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::St.summary: Full station data including resolved calls, callers, and explanation.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::St.explanation: The full response shape for the `/station/:id` endpoint, combining all index fields for a station with its resolved inbound and outbound connections and its parent line's summary. The `explanation` field is the richer multi-sentence description generated at index time, as opposed to `summary` which is one sentence. The key related type is `ResolvedConnection`, which populates the `calls` and `called_by` vectors.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.name: ParsedFunction
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.summary: Holds a parsed function or type item with its ID, location, body, and call list.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.explanation: The central intermediate representation for a single indexable code item — a function, method, struct, or enum — holding its source body, location, and best-effort call list. The `body_hash` method on this struct is what drives the incremental cache in `generate_summaries`. Fields map directly onto `Station` fields in `metroscope-types`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::mani.name: manifest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::mani.summary: Returns a JSON manifest describing all available hayai tool endpoints.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::mani.explanation: Returns a static JSON document describing all available HTTP tools, acting as a machine-readable API index for agents or humans discovering the server. It's served at `GET /` and doesn't touch the store. The descriptions here are what an orchestrating AI would read to know which endpoint to call.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Esca.name: EscalateRequest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Esca.summary: Request payload carrying a fork ID and description for escalation.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Esca.explanation: A plain data transfer struct carrying the fork ID and a human-readable description when a fork needs to be escalated to a human or higher-level orchestrator. It exists solely to deserialize the JSON body of POST requests to the escalate endpoint; no logic lives here. The description field becomes an open question in the shared context. Its only consumer is `escalate_fork`.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Co.name: ContentBlock
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Co.summary: Wraps a single text string from a Claude API response content array.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Co.explanation: Represents one element of the `content` array in the API response, which can technically contain multiple blocks. The indexer joins all block texts with an empty separator, treating multi-block responses as a single string. In practice Claude returns a single text block, but this handles edge cases without error.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: render_svg
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Renders the full index as an SVG diagram with crate panels and arrows.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: The single public function in the export module; it takes a fully-built `Index` and produces a complete SVG document as a string. It runs in two passes — first a layout pass that computes positions and box heights, then a draw pass that emits crate panels, file labels, station boxes, and Bézier call arrows — and it is the only caller of every other function in the file.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.name: ApiResponse
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.summary: Deserializes the Claude API response body containing content blocks and token usage.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Ap.explanation: Maps the top-level shape of a successful Anthropic API response. Only `content` and `usage` are captured; other fields like `id` and `model` are intentionally ignored since the indexer doesn't need them. `call_via_api` deserializes into this after confirming a 2xx status.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: get_quest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Returns a quest and its associated forks by quest ID.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Fetches a single quest and its associated forks in one response, avoiding the need to deserialize the entire tree. The fork hydration is done inline by iterating `quest.fork_ids` and looking each up in `tree.forks`, so forks that were deleted but still referenced by the quest will silently disappear from the result via `filter_map`. Callers that need worker details must follow up with a separate lookup.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.name: try_resolve_fork
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.summary: Asks the model to choose a fork option or escalate the decision to a human.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.explanation: Presents a structured decision question and its labeled options to the model and parses out a RESOLVE <id> response if the model expresses confidence, otherwise returning None to signal that a human should decide. The option-id validation step is important: it prevents the model from inventing a new option that doesn't exist in the fork's actual list. The result feeds back into the fork resolution logic in the MCP server handlers.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.name: get_tree
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.summary: Returns the full skill tree (acts, quests, forks) as JSON.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.explanation: Dumps the entire skill tree — acts, quests, and forks — as a single JSON blob under a read lock. Useful for getting the full picture at once, though for large trees the per-quest endpoint is more targeted. Both this and `get_context` are read-only and never mutate state.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.name: ProposeForkOption
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.summary: A single fork option with label, description, unlocks, and forecloses fields.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.explanation: One branch in a proposed fork, describing what choosing it would unlock versus foreclose. The `unlocks` and `forecloses` fields are free-form strings rather than structured references, so they're documentation for the AI rather than enforced graph edges.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Cro.name: CrossConnection
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Cro.summary: Holds a directed cross-file connection between two stations with its kind.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Cro.explanation: A plain data container representing a single cross-file connection between two functions, capturing the source station name, the target station name, and the direction of the relationship. It exists as the atomic unit of cross-file dependency information that gets grouped and surfaced by `build_file_connections`. The `kind` field distinguishes whether the calling direction is outbound ("calls") or inbound ("called_by"), which determines which bucket it lands in downstream.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::noti.name: notify_change
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::noti.summary: Logs a file change and checks Ollama whether it is relevant to the active quest.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::noti.explanation: Records a file-change event, maintains a sliding window of the ten most recently touched files in the shared context, and then asks the local Ollama model whether the changed file is relevant to the currently active quest and its pending forks. The relevance check is best-effort — a failure defaults to `false` rather than blocking the response. The handler currently only logs relevant changes, leaving a marked comment where future orchestration logic would trigger follow-up work.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.name: handle_module_map
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.summary: Returns the crate-level module map for the entire index.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.explanation: Handles GET `/module-map` and returns a crate-level overview by delegating to `map::build_module_map`. It takes no query parameters — the module map is always the full project view. Follow `map::build_module_map` to understand how crates are grouped and summarized.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Sta.name: StationView
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Sta.summary: A station's display data including focus and cross-line connection flag.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Sta.explanation: A response-ready station entry inside a `LineView`, with `is_focused` and `has_cross_line` flags pre-computed so the client doesn't need to reason about connections. The `has_cross_line` flag is the most expensive field to compute — it triggers a full connection scan via `station_has_cross_line`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::mai.name: main
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::mai.summary: Parses CLI args and dispatches to init, serve, or notify-change handlers.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::mai.explanation: Entry point that parses the CLI and dispatches to one of three handlers: `init::run` for tree bootstrapping, `serve` for the MCP server lifecycle, or `notify_change` for the hook client. Tracing is initialized before dispatch so all three paths get structured logging. The next place to look is the `serve` function, which wires up the `Store` and `HayaiMcpServer`.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.name: build_explanation_batch_prompt
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.summary: Builds a prompt requesting 2-4 sentence explanations for a batch of functions.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.explanation: Builds the prompt for the explanation pass, which asks for 2-4 sentence descriptions rather than one-liners, using the same `id|content` response format as the summary pass. It is structurally identical to `build_function_batch_prompt` but with a richer instruction header and no override mechanism. Its output is parsed by `parse_function_batch_response`.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.name: handle_map
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.summary: Returns a map response focused on the station at the given file and line.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.explanation: Handles GET `/map` by acquiring a read lock on the index, resolving the focused station from the file+line query params, and delegating to `map::build_map_response`. The read lock is held only for the duration of the handler, keeping contention with the file watcher low. The key callee to follow is `map::build_map_response`, which contains the actual windowing and grouping logic.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.name: handle_connections
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.summary: Returns cross-file connection summary for the given file.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::ha.explanation: Handles GET `/connections` by delegating entirely to `map::build_file_connections`, which computes which other files the given file calls into or is called from. It's a thin adapter between the HTTP layer and the map logic; all the interesting work is in `map::build_file_connections`.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Conn.name: ConnectionKind
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Conn.summary: Distinguishes whether a connection is a call made or a call received.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Conn.explanation: Distinguishes the direction of a call relationship between stations. `Calls` is populated by the tree-sitter parser at index time, while `CalledBy` is added later by the optional Serena MCP enrichment pass. Keeping direction explicit means the server can answer both "what does this call?" and "what calls this?" from a single flat `connections` list.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Ques.name: Quest
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Ques.summary: Represents an architectural improvement suggestion with title, component, rationale, and difficulty.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Ques.explanation: Represents a single LLM-generated architectural improvement suggestion for the project. Each quest is scoped to either a specific crate or the whole system via `component`, and carries a human-readable rationale in `why` so the developer immediately understands the motivation. Quests are generated once at index time and stored in `Index.quests`; no runtime LLM calls are needed to display them.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::AppS.name: AppState
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::AppS.summary: Shared server state holding the store and Ollama client.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::AppS.explanation: Shared server state injected into every HTTP route handler via axum's `State` extractor. It bundles the persistent `Store` and the `OllamaClient` together so handlers can access both without globals. The `Arc` wrapping on both fields means the state is cheaply cloned per request without copying the underlying data.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Disp.name: DispatchWorkerRequest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Disp.summary: Request payload carrying a fork ID and instructions for a new worker.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Disp.explanation: A request payload struct pairing a fork ID with the instructions to hand off to a new worker. It exists to deserialize the JSON body sent to the dispatch endpoint and carries no behavior of its own. `dispatch_worker` is its sole consumer.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Qu.name: QuestStatus
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Qu.summary: Tracks quest progression through locked, available, in-progress, and complete states.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Qu.explanation: A four-state lifecycle enum for individual quests, progressing from `Locked` (dependencies not met) through `Available` (dependencies met, not yet started) and `InProgress` (a worker is running) to `Complete`. The orchestrator reads this to decide whether to dispatch a new worker or wait, and `StateEvent::QuestComplete` is the trigger that advances it to `Complete`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::rebu.name: rebuild_context
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::rebu.summary: Recomputes active act, quest, and fork ID lists from current tree state.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::rebu.explanation: Derives a fresh snapshot of high-level orchestration state — current act number, active quest, pending forks, and escalated forks — by scanning the authoritative tree and writing the results into the context store. It drops the tree read lock before acquiring the context write lock to avoid deadlock. This function is called by `worker_done` after any state transition that could change which quest or forks are active.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ge.name: generate_summaries
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ge.summary: Generates function summaries, explanations, file summaries, system summary, and architectural quests via batched LLM calls, with caching.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::ge.explanation: Orchestrates the entire LLM summarization pipeline for an index run: it generates per-function summaries, per-function explanations, per-file summaries, a system-wide summary, and architectural quests. It skips functions whose body hash matches the cache to avoid redundant API calls. The output feeds directly into the index-building step in `main.rs`, and the individual prompt builders (`build_function_batch_prompt`, `build_explanation_batch_prompt`, etc.) are the next place to look for how each pass is structured.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::St.name: StateEvent
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::St.summary: Enumerates all state-changing events that can occur in the hayai system.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::St.explanation: The sealed event vocabulary that drives all state transitions in the hayai system — every mutation to `Tree` or `Context` is expressed as one of these variants rather than as direct field writes.
