This makes the event log replayable and auditable. `ForkProposed` and `ForkEscalated` are the two paths a new decision point can take; `QuestComplete` and `ActComplete` advance the DAG; `FileChanged` and `ReIndexed` connect the planning system to the live codebase state tracked by the indexer.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::sta.name: station_has_cross_line
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::sta.summary: Returns true if the station connects to any station on a different line.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::sta.explanation: Determines whether a station has any connections that cross line (file) boundaries, which the UI uses to draw interchange markers. It handles two connection formats: Serena-enriched connections that store full station IDs (fast O(1) lookup), and tree-sitter connections that only store a bare function name (requires scanning all stations by name). The name-only fallback is a potential performance hotspot on large indexes.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::not.name: notify_change
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::not.summary: POSTs a file-change notification to the hayai server's notify-change endpoint.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs::not.explanation: A thin CLI subcommand that fires a POST to a running Hayai server to signal that a file was edited. It exists so a PostToolUse hook in Claude Code can ping the server without needing any native MCP machinery. There are no retries or error surfacing beyond the `?` propagation — if the server isn't running, the hook silently fails. The receiving end is the `notify_change` handler in `mcp.rs`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.name: ForkOption
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.summary: Represents one selectable branch in a fork with downstream impact descriptions.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Fo.explanation: Describes one branch of a decision point in human-readable terms, including what downstream work it enables (`unlocks`) and what it forecloses. The `id` field is what gets stored in `Fork.chosen_option_id` when the fork is resolved, linking the choice back to this option's consequences.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.name: build_file_prompt
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.summary: Builds a prompt requesting a one-sentence summary for a single source file.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::bu.explanation: Constructs the prompt for summarizing a single file, including the file ID, function names, and up to 6000 characters of source. The 6000-character truncation is a hard cap to avoid token overruns on large files. Called in the file-summarization loop inside `generate_summaries`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: rebuild_context
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Recomputes and overwrites context from current tree state.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Recomputes the derived context snapshot from the authoritative tree state — finding the active act, active quest, and all pending/escalated fork IDs. It intentionally drops the tree read lock before acquiring the context write lock to prevent a deadlock. This function is called after `worker_done` because completing a worker can cascade into quest completion, which changes what the context should reflect.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.name: MetroIndex
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.summary: Holds system summary and list of quests parsed from the Metroscope index.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.explanation: A deserialization target for `index.json` produced by the metroscope indexer, capturing the project-level system summary and a flat list of recommended quests. It exists only during the `init` bootstrap phase — it's never written back to disk and is converted immediately into the richer `TreeQuest` format. Look at `run` to see how its fields are consumed.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.name: SerenaIndex
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.summary: Maps function IDs to their lists of callers.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs:.explanation: A flat map from a function's station ID to all the callers that reference it, collected after querying Serena for every function in the index. It acts as the transfer object between `enrich` and `index_project`, which iterates over it to push `CalledBy` connections onto stations.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.name: get_context
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.summary: Returns the current session context as JSON.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.explanation: Serializes the current `Context` — active quest, current act, pending forks — as JSON under a read lock. It's intended to be called at the start of every Claude Code session to re-establish situational awareness. The `.unwrap_or_default()` on serialization means a broken context silently returns `null` rather than a 500.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.name: get_quest
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.summary: Returns a quest and its associated forks by ID, or 404 if not found.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.explanation: Fetches a single quest by ID and inlines its associated fork objects, so callers get a self-contained view without needing a second request. It reads fork details by walking `quest.fork_ids` and looking each up in `tree.forks` — forks that have been deleted or never inserted are silently skipped by `filter_map`. Returns 404 if the quest ID doesn't exist.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.name: kind_badge
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.summary: Returns a short badge string for a station's kind.
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs::.explanation: Maps a `StationKind` variant to a short ASCII label used as the badge in the top-right corner of each station box in the SVG. The single-character or two-character strings are chosen to fit in the reserved space without truncation.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.name: ProposeForkResponse
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.summary: Response containing the newly created fork's ID.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::Prop.explanation: Minimal response returned after a fork is created, containing only the new fork's UUID. The caller uses this ID for subsequent `resolve_fork` or `escalate` calls — keeping it small avoids re-serializing the entire fork object on creation.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.name: save_context
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.summary: Updates the context timestamp and serializes it to `context.json`.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.explanation: Acquires a write lock on context, stamps updated_at with the current UTC time, then flushes to context.json. The timestamp update is the reason a write lock is needed even though the caller may not have changed any other field. It mirrors save_tree and is called by MCP handlers after mutations to quests, forks, or other context fields.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.name: new
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.summary: Constructs a `RustParser` with the tree-sitter Rust language loaded.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.explanation: Initializes the parser by converting the static tree-sitter-rust grammar into a `Language` handle. It exists as a constructor so callers don't depend on the `tree_sitter_rust` crate directly. The returned instance is passed to `LanguageParser::parse` to process individual files.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.name: get_escalations
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.summary: Returns all forks with escalated status from the tree.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::get_.explanation: A read-only endpoint that filters the fork tree for any fork whose status is `Escalated` and returns them as a JSON array. It holds only a read lock, so it never blocks writers. This is the polling surface for an orchestrator or human operator to discover what needs their attention before work can resume.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Mod.name: ModuleMap
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Mod.summary: Holds all module nodes, system summary, and project root path.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Mod.explanation: The top-level response type for the `/module-map` endpoint, wrapping the list of `ModuleNode`s together with the project-wide system summary and the project root path. The system summary comes directly from the index (written by the indexer's Claude API call) and is displayed as the header text in the Neovim module map view.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Su.name: Summaries
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Su.summary: Collects all LLM-generated summaries, explanations, quests, and token usage counts.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs::Su.explanation: Collects all LLM outputs from a single indexing run into one place. Keeping token counts here lets the CLI report total API cost at the end without threading counters through every call site. The `quests` field is populated by the final system-level prompt, while the two function maps are built up incrementally across the per-function batch calls.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::lo.name: load_index
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::lo.summary: Reads and deserializes the index JSON file into an Index.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::lo.explanation: Reads the index.json file from disk and deserializes it into an `Index` struct using serde_json. It exists as a standalone function because it's called both at startup and on every hot-reload triggered by the file watcher. The error context messages are carefully worded to distinguish read failures from parse failures, which matters when debugging a corrupt index file. The primary caller is `main`, and the secondary caller is `watch_index`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::disp.name: dispatch_worker
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::disp.summary: Creates a running worker for a fork and records it in the tree.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs::disp.explanation: Creates a new `Worker` record with a fresh UUID, sets it to `Running`, links it to its parent fork by storing the worker ID on the fork, and persists both the tree and a log event. It does not actually spawn any asynchronous task — the "worker" is a logical record that an external agent (another process or Claude sub-agent) will fulfill and later report back via `worker_done`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Wo.name: Worker
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Wo.summary: Represents a dispatched worker handling a fork with instructions and completion state.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs::Wo.explanation: Represents a spawned agent instance tied to a specific fork, carrying the instructions it was given and, once finished, a summary of what it did. Workers are created in response to a fork being resolved, and their completion via `StateEvent::WorkerDone` feeds the summary back to the orchestrator to inform subsequent decisions. The `finished_at` timestamp and `WorkerStatus` together tell the orchestrator the worker's current state.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Inde.name: Index
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Inde.summary: Top-level project index holding all stations, lines, entry points, and quests.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Inde.explanation: The complete serialized snapshot of a project — everything the server needs, loaded once at startup from `.metroscope/index.json`. Using `HashMap` for both `stations` and `lines` means O(1) lookup by ID at query time, which matters since every HTTP request resolves multiple IDs. `quests` defaults to an empty vec via `serde(default)` so old index files without that field still deserialize cleanly.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: worker_done
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Marks a worker done, resolves its fork, and completes the quest if all forks are resolved.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.explanation: Marks the worker as finished, auto-resolves its parent fork if not already resolved, and checks whether all forks in the quest are now resolved to potentially mark the quest complete. The scope-bounded `tree` write lock is dropped before calling `rebuild_context`, which avoids a deadlock since `rebuild_context` also acquires a read lock on the tree. This is the only mutation path that triggers a full `rebuild_context` call.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.name: impl_type_name
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.summary: Walks up the AST to find the `impl` block type name for a method node.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.explanation: Walks up the tree-sitter node ancestry from a function node to find an enclosing `impl_item`, then extracts the type name from that impl block. This is how the parser distinguishes free functions from methods and assigns the correct owner for ID generation. Its result is consumed directly in `extract_functions`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.name: as_str
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.summary: Returns the lowercase string representation of the difficulty variant.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs::Met.explanation: Converts the difficulty enum to a lowercase string for embedding into quest description text. It returns a `&'static str` rather than implementing `Display` or `serde::Serialize` because it's only ever used in one format string inside `run` and doesn't need general-purpose serialization.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Co.name: ConnectionsParams
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Co.summary: Query parameter struct holding the file ID for connection lookup.
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs::Co.explanation: A single-field query param struct for the `/connections` endpoint, requiring a `file` identifier. It exists to give the handler a typed, validated query string rather than raw string extraction. The file ID here is a relative path used as a line ID, matching the convention throughout the index.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.name: load
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.summary: Loads tree and context from JSON files, creating the directory if needed.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs::St.explanation: Initializes a Store from a `.metro/` directory, creating the directory if it doesn't exist and silently defaulting to empty values if either JSON file is missing or corrupt. The unwrap_or_default fallback means a fresh project starts with zero state rather than an error, which is the intended bootstrap behavior. After load, callers typically call save_tree or save_context to persist any changes made through the lock guards.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Ques.name: QuestDifficulty
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Ques.summary: Classifies an architectural improvement quest as easy, medium, or hard.
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs::Ques.explanation: A simple three-level effort estimate attached to each Quest suggestion. It exists so the Neovim plugin or a future UI can sort or filter quests by how much work they imply. The LLM assigns this value at index time as part of the architectural suggestion batch.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/color.rs::.name: for_file
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/color.rs::.summary: Returns a stable palette color for a file by hashing its ID.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/color.rs::.explanation: Deterministically assigns a color from a fixed palette to a file by FNV-1a hashing the file ID string. The same file always gets the same color across re-indexes because the hash is purely content-derived. This is called during index building to populate each station's display color in the map view.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Mod.name: ModuleNode
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Mod.summary: Represents a crate module with its connections to other modules.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::Mod.explanation: Represents a single crate in the module-level overview, bundling display metadata (name, color, summary) with aggregated statistics (station count) and deduplicated cross-crate edge lists. The `calls_into` and `called_from` fields hold crate IDs, not file or station IDs, making this a coarser view than `FileConnections`. It is assembled by `build_module_map` and serialized into `ModuleMap` for the `/module-map` endpoint.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.name: parse
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.summary: Parses a Rust source file with tree-sitter and extracts all functions and types.
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.explanation: Drives the full parse pipeline for one source file: initializes a tree-sitter parser, parses the source into a CST, disambiguates the display name, and delegates item extraction to `extract_functions`. The `ParsedFile` it returns is the primary input to `generate_summaries`.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.name: new
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.summary: Constructs a new `OllamaClient` with the given base URL and model name.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.explanation: Plain constructor that moves the base URL and model name into the struct and creates a default reqwest Client. No validation is performed on the URL or model name at construction time; errors surface at the first generate call. The entry point that creates this is wherever the hayai binary or MCP server initializes its Ollama integration.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.name: generate
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.summary: Sends a prompt to Ollama and returns the generated text response.
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs::O.explanation: Posts a prompt to Ollama's /api/generate endpoint with streaming disabled and returns the response text. It calls error_for_status so any non-2xx HTTP response becomes an immediate error rather than a silent empty string. This is the single low-level I/O method that all higher-level methods — is_relevant and try_resolve_fork — ultimately delegate to.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::cra.name: crate_id_of
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::cra.summary: Extracts the crate name from a file path, returning "root" if outside crates/.
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs::cra.explanation: Extracts a stable crate identifier from a file's path by checking whether the path follows the `crates/<name>/src/...` workspace layout. Files outside that pattern — such as top-level scripts or non-workspace code — fall back to the string `"root"`. Both `build_file_connections` and `build_module_map` call this to decide whether two stations live in the same crate before recording cross-crate edges.

crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs.name: server/map
crates/metroscope-server/src/map.rs.summary: Builds map, module-map, and file-connection response structures from the index for the HTTP server endpoints.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs.name: hayai/ollama
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/ollama.rs.summary: Wraps an Ollama HTTP client to generate text, classify file-change relevance, and auto-resolve decision forks.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs.name: hayai/mcp
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp.rs.summary: Implements the axum HTTP MCP server exposing tools for quest, fork, and worker orchestration to Claude Code.

crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs.name: types/lib
crates/metroscope-types/src/lib.rs.summary: Defines shared data types (`Station`, `Line`, `Index`, `Connection`, `Quest`) serialized to the project index JSON.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs.name: hayai/types
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/types.rs.summary: Defines shared data types for a quest/act skill-tree system, including acts, quests, forks, workers, and state events.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs.name: hayai/store
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/store.rs.summary: Manages async-safe persistent storage for tree, context, and append-only state log in a `.metro` directory.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.name: rust
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/rus.summary: Parses Rust source files using tree-sitter to extract structs, enums, functions, and methods with their locations and call sites.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs.name: indexer/serena
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/serena.rs.summary: Spawns a Serena MCP server over stdio and queries it to enrich the index with LSP-accurate caller relationships per function.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs.name: indexer/main
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/main.rs.summary: Parses Rust projects, extracts functions via tree-sitter, generates LLM summaries, and writes a `.metroscope/index.json` file.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs.name: indexer/llm
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/llm.rs.summary: Calls Claude API or CLI to generate per-function, per-file, and system-level summaries for parsed Rust source files.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.name: hayai/mcp_server
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/mcp_server.r.summary: Implements the rmcp MCP server exposing Hayai quest/fork/worker management tools over HTTP to Claude Code.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs.name: hayai/init
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/init.rs.summary: Bootstraps a Hayai skill tree by reading a Metroscope index and converting its quests into Act 1 TreeQuests.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/color.rs.name: indexer/color
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/color.rs.summary: Assigns deterministic hex colors to file paths using FNV hash and a fixed metro-style palette.

crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs.name: server/export
crates/metroscope-server/src/export.rs.summary: Renders the metroscope index as an SVG diagram with crate boxes, station cards, and connection arrows.

crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs.name: server/main
crates/metroscope-server/src/main.rs.summary: Serves the metroscope HTTP API, loading and hot-reloading the index, and routing requests to map, station, connections, and export handlers.

crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs.name: hayai/main
crates/metroscope-hayai/src/main.rs.summary: Hayai's CLI entry point: starts an MCP/HTTP server, initializes the skill tree, or notifies the server of file changes.

crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.name: mod
crates/metroscope-indexer/src/parser/mod.summary: Defines `ParsedFunction` and `ParsedFile` data structures and the `LanguageParser` trait for extracting code symbols from source files.

quests.title: Add /health endpoint for server readiness
quests.why: Editor plugins and process supervisors need a way to probe liveness before making map requests. Without it, clients can't distinguish a starting server from a dead one, causing silent failures on open.

quests.title: Replace permissive CORS with auth middleware
quests.why: All four API endpoints are publicly accessible with no authentication. Any process on the host can read the full code index, which may contain proprietary logic and LLM-generated summaries.

quests.title: Atomic index writes with tmp-file swap
quests.why: The indexer writes directly to `.metroscope/index.json` mid-run; a crash or SIGINT produces a corrupted index with no recovery path. A write-to-temp-then-rename pattern gives atomic, crash-safe persistence at zero cost.

quests.title: Add Claude API rate limiting and retry
quests.why: The indexer fires batches against the Claude API with no backoff or throttle. Under large codebases this will hit rate limits silently and either drop summaries or crash the run, with no way to resume from the failure point.

quests.title: Unify structured logging across all crates
quests.why: `metroscope-server` and `metroscope-indexer` use raw `println!`/`eprintln!`; only hayai uses `tracing`. Operators (and the hayai LLM context) have no machine-readable log stream to diagnose indexing failures or server errors.

quests.title: Add Hayai state replay and crash recovery
quests.why: The append-only `state.log` exists but there is no replay logic on startup — a crashed hayai process loses all in-flight quest/worker state. Replaying the log on boot would make the skill tree resilient to restarts without needing a full database.

quests.title: Add incremental re-indexing triggered by file changes
quests.why: The indexer must be run manually and re-processes the whole project even when only one file changed. Wiring the hayai file-change notification path through to a targeted re-index of only dirty files would make the index stay current automatically.
